title: Claim Ledger — FlashAttention-4 White-Box Runtime Model
body:
# Claim Ledger — FlashAttention-4 White-Box Runtime Model

| Claim | Status | Evidence | Caveats |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Final refined model achieves 12.62% validation MAPE and 10.01% query MAPE on real B200 | supported | Table 2, real-hardware measurements | Synthetic matrix; bf16/fp16/fp8 only |
| ≥90% of configurations are within 30% absolute error | supported | Table 2 | Same matrix limits; small-runtime cases can exceed 30% APE |
| NCU bottleneck accuracy is 100% on validation and query | supported | Section 3.6, NCU profiles | Every NCU profile in this dataset is compute-bound; slack not validated on memory-bound cases |
| Launch/grid overhead is the dominant correction when moving from emulator to B200 | supported | First real-hardware pass vs. improved pass | Fixed overhead value calibrated on this B200/driver stack |
| NCU-guided slack gamma=3.0 closes the bottleneck-label gap while preserving runtime MAPE | supported | Section 3.6, Table 2 | Only changes label, not runtime estimate |
| Model generalises beyond the studied matrix | unsupported | Matrix omits custom tile sizes, num_splits, fused variants, production workloads | Explicit limitation in Section 6 |
| Model supports fp4 | unsupported | Installed FA4 build does not support fp4 | Explicit limitation in Section 6 |
| Calibrated slack generalises to memory-bound NCU regimes | unsupported | No memory-bound NCU profiles in dataset | Explicit limitation in Section 6 |
| White-box predictor is useful for kernel design | partially supported | Interpretable bottleneck labels and factors | Utility not tested in a real design loop |
